howItWorks.intro: This rv campground cost calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total = (Base Nightly Rate + Hookup Surcharge) × Number of Nights
Full hookups typically add $5-15/night over dry camping
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How much does an RV campsite cost per night?
faq.answer: Average RV campsite costs range from $25-50 for basic sites to $50-100+ for premium RV resorts with full hookups. National parks average $25-35. Boondocking (dry camping) on public land is often free.

faq.question: What are full hookups?
faq.answer: Full hookups include water, electric (30 or 50 amp), and sewer connections at your campsite. This lets you use all RV systems without relying on onboard tanks or a generator.

faq.question: How can I save on RV camping costs?
faq.answer: Book weekly or monthly rates for 15-30% savings. Use Harvest Hosts, Boondockers Welcome, or free BLM land. Get a Thousand Trails membership for unlimited nights. Camp mid-week for lower rates.
